title: Fixing CSS Media Query Priority with Inconsistent Feature Ordering

statement.markdown: Write a function that parses a CSS rule and determines the order of media features in a media query. Given a CSS rule string, identify the media query and extract the media features. If the media features are in the correct order, return `true`; otherwise, return `false`. For example, if the input CSS rule is `@media (max-width: 768px) and (min-height: 600px) { /* styles */ }`, the function should return `true` because the media features are in ascending order of precedence.
statement.inputFormat: A string representing a CSS rule
statement.outputFormat: A boolean indicating whether the media features are in the correct order
statement.notes: The input string will always contain a media query. The media features will be separated by the `and` keyword.

constraints.description: Length of the input CSS rule string

examples.explanation: The media features are in ascending order of precedence.
The media features are not in ascending order of precedence.

hints.text: Extract the media query from the CSS rule string.

editorial.title: Fixing CSS Media Query Priority with Inconsistent Feature Ordering
editorial.intuition: The correct order of media features is determined by their precedence. The precedence of media features is as follows: `not`, `only`, `and`, `or`, and `not`.
editorial.bruteForce: One possible approach is to parse the media query string and check the order of media features manually.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use a regular expression to extract the media features and then compare their order.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness is based on the fact that the media features are extracted in the correct order and compared using the precedence rules.
commonMistakes: Not extracting the media query correctly
Not comparing the media features correctly

starterCode.javascript: // function that parses a CSS rule and determines the order of media features in a media query
starterCode.python: // class that parses a CSS rule and determines the order of media features in a media query
